key_insight: The paper introduces a configurable chemical validity operator that filters and guides AI-generated inorganic materials using oxidation-state-aware chemical rules.
engineering_relevance: It improves the reliability of generative materials design by rejecting implausible compositions while preserving promising low-energy candidates for faster screening workflows.
method: Built on SMACT with a data-informed oxidation-state model and tunable thresholds.
Applied the operator to benchmark six generative crystal models for chemical validity screening.
Used the same filter as a reinforcement-learning reward to steer a latent diffusion model.
evidence_signal: In benchmarking, most models matched stoichiometry but often missed realistic oxidation-state combinations; filtering removed compositions with rarely observed oxidation states while keeping compounds near the convex hull.
watch_item: Because the filter depends on configurable chemical heuristics and observed oxidation-state data, overly strict thresholds could exclude novel but potentially valid materials.
paper_title: Chemical filters for ultra-high-throughput materials screening and generation
